query: United Russia 2026 congress federal candidate list Medvedev June 2026
answer:
This is excellent — the agent found comprehensive research. Let me compile the final synthesis.

---

## Research Report: United Russia 2026 Congress, Federal Candidate List & Medvedev

### 1. 2026 Russian State Duma Election Schedule

The **2026 Russian State Duma elections are scheduled for September 20, 2026**. All 450 seats are contested under a mixed (parallel) voting system:
- **225 seats** via party-list proportional representation (5% threshold)
- **225 seats** in single-member districts (SMDs)

Under current law (amended April 2021), the **all-federal part** of a party's candidate list may include **up to 15 candidates** (raised from the previous cap of 10).

### 2. United Russia's Pre-Election Congress — June 28, 2026

**United Russia's pre-election congress is reportedly scheduled for June 28, 2026** — approximately 10 days from now. This is the event where the party's federal candidate list, including the crucial all-federal part, will be formally approved.

**Supporting evidence:**
- **Historical precedent:** In 2021, United Russia's pre-election congress was held on **June 19, 2021**, ~3 months before the September vote.
- **Other parties' congresses fall in the same window:** Yabloko's pre-election congress is set for **June 27, 2026** (Vedomosti, June 16, 2026).
- **United Russia's primaries concluded May 25–31, 2026** (conducted fully electronically for the first time), a prerequisite step before the congress.

### 3. Federal Candidate List — Current Status (As of mid-June 2026)

**The specific names composing the all-federal part of United Russia's list have NOT yet been publicly announced.** The congress is where this list will be revealed.

| Detail | Information |
|---|---|
| **Campaign HQ Head** | **Vladimir Yakushev**, Secretary of UR General Council (appointed Jan 2026, per RBC) |
| **Faction Renewal** | Yakushev announced "significant rotation" — many current deputies may be replaced (TASS, Dec 2025) |
| **Deputy Evaluation** | Presidential Administration uses a "traffic light system" to assess sitting deputies |
| **Primaries** | May 25–31, 2026; fully electronic; selected constituency and regional list candidates |
| **Current seat count** | United Russia holds **315–323 seats** (constitutional supermajority) |

**Constituency changes:** New constituencies were created in four Russian-occupied Ukrainian regions (Donetsk, Luhansk, Kherson, Zaporizhzhia), adding 7 seats while eliminating 10 elsewhere.

### 4. Dmitry Medvedev's Role and Position

**Whether Medvedev will appear in the 2026 all-federal part is not yet publicly known.** Key context:

| Election | Medvedev's Role on UR List | UR List Vote % | UR Total Seats |
|----------|---------------------------|----------------|----------------|
| **2011** | **Headed the list** (sole locomotive; then President) | 49.3% | 238 |
| **2016** | **Headed the list** (then PM and party chairman) | 54.2% | 343 |
| **2021** | **Excluded** from top of list | 49.8% | 324 |

- In **2021**, Medvedev was notably **excluded** from the top of the list. Putin instead proposed 5 individuals: Sergei Shoigu, Sergey Lavrov, Denis Protsenko, Yelena Shmelyova, and Anna Kuznetsova. This reflected Medvedev's **declining popularity** following pension reform backlash.
- Medvedev **remains party chairman** of United Russia and serves as **Deputy Chairman of the Security Council**.
- His hawkish rhetoric during the Russia-Ukraine war (since 2022) has reshaped his public profile compared to his earlier moderate technocratic image.

### 5. Historical All-Federal Part Composition

| Election | Leader(s) | # in All-Federal Part | Model |
|----------|-----------|----------------------|-------|
| **2007** | Putin (sole) | 1 | Single locomotive |
| **2011** | Medvedev (sole) | 1 | Single locomotive |
| **2016** | Medvedev | ~1–3 | Single locomotive |
| **2021** | Shoigu, Lavrov, Protsenko, Kuznetsova, Shmeleva | 5 | Collective model |
| **2026** | **TBD (June 28 congress)** | Up to 15 | Unknown |

**Key trend:** The party shifted from single "locomotive" figures (2007–2016) to a collective model (2021). None of the all-federal part leaders in any cycle (2007–2021) actually took up Duma seats — they remained in executive roles.

### 6. Comparative Context — Other Parties

- **New People** already approved their top 3 federal list candidates (Nechayev, Davankov, Avksentyeva) at a **March 2026** congress.
- **Yabloko's** pre-election congress is set for **June 27, 2026**.
- Putin stated in **April 2026** that "elections to the State Duma will take place in difficult conditions."

### Key Open Questions

1. **Will Putin propose or lead the United Russia list?** He did not in 2021 but did in 2007 and 2011.
2. **Will Medvedev return to the all-federal part?** He was excluded in 2021 after heading it in 2011 and 2016.
3. **What is the composition of the all-federal part?** Up to 15 names, to be revealed at the June 28 congress.
4. **Will the 2021 collective model be repeated or expanded?** The new 15-candidate cap allows larger lists.

The all-federal part of a party's candidate list may include up to 15 candidates under current law (raised from 10).
As of mid-June 2026, the specific names composing United Russia's all-federal list have NOT been publicly announced.
Dmitry Medvedev remains United Russia party chairman and Deputy Chairman of the Security Council.
Medvedev was EXCLUDED from the all-federal part in 2021 after heading the list in 2011 and 2016, reflecting his declining popularity after pension reform backlash.
In 2021, Putin proposed 5 collective 'locomotive' figures (Shoigu, Lavrov, Protsenko, Shmelyova, Kuznetsova) instead of a single leader.
Vladimir Yakushev was appointed head of United Russia's campaign HQ in January 2026 and announced 'significant rotation' of Duma deputies.
United Russia currently holds 315-323 seats, a constitutional supermajority.
New constituencies were created in four Russian-occupied Ukrainian regions (Donetsk, Luhansk, Kherson, Zaporizhzhia), adding 7 seats while eliminating 10 elsewhere.
